title: Natural air freshener gel
subtitle: A gelatin-set room fragrance gel with essential oils for a slow, continuous scent release
excerpt: A gelatin gel holds water and releases fragrance slowly as the surface evaporates, giving a gentler and longer-lasting room scent than a spray. Set the gel in a wide-mouthed glass jar so the surface area is large. One jar lasts 3 to 4 weeks. The scent blend here is eucalyptus, lemon, and lavender: clean and household-neutral.
sourceNotes: Gelatin-based air freshener gel: standard formula using unflavoured gelatin (2 sachets per 250 ml water) plus salt to slow evaporation. Salt at 1 tsp per 250 ml water slows microbial growth and reduces evaporation rate for longer-lasting gel. Essential oils at approximately 1% of water volume. Colourant optional (cosmetic-grade liquid or gel). Shelf life: 3 to 4 weeks at room temperature.

recipe.batchNotes: Scale proportionally. Pour into multiple jars from one batch.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Allow 4 hours to set fully before placing in a room.
recipe.temperatureNote: Heat water to 80°C to dissolve gelatin fully. Allow to cool to 50°C before adding essential oils.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: 3 to 4 weeks at room temperature. Refrigerate unused jars to extend. Discard when the gel has dried out or when all scent has dissipated.

glossaryTerms.definition: A protein-based gel formed by dissolving unflavoured gelatin in hot water and allowing it to set. In an air freshener the water evaporates slowly through the gel surface, carrying fragrance compounds into the air.
How far and how strongly a fragrance source projects its scent into a room. Gels have a softer, more ambient scent throw than sprays; they suit smaller rooms and enclosed spaces.

content.text: holds essential oils suspended in a slow-evaporating water matrix, giving a sustained

that a spray cannot match. The gel is placed in any room; no electricity, no batteries, no respraying. A wide-mouthed jar maximises surface area and evaporation. Essential oils go in once the liquid has cooled to 50°C; adding them to hot liquid volatilises and wastes them.

items.prepNote: 2 standard sachets (7 g each); or equivalent leaf gelatin
Slows evaporation and inhibits microbial growth
Add at 50°C; not to hot liquid
Optional; cosmetic-grade only; add with essential oils

content.text: Bring the water to 80°C in a pan or beaker. Remove from heat. Sprinkle the gelatin over the hot water and stir continuously for 2 minutes until completely dissolved with no lumps. Add salt and stir.

Allow the liquid to cool to 50°C, checking with the probe thermometer. Add essential oils and optional colourant. Stir gently. Pour into a clean glass jar. Leave at room temperature to set for at least 4 hours.
3 to 4 weeks at room temperature once opened. Unused jars can be sealed with cling film and refrigerated for up to 3 months.
